title: Flaky Comforting American Perilla-Leaf-Kkaennip Dumplings
description: Pungent, flaky, comforting American dish featuring perilla-leaf-kkaennip, dum, and nut-free-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Combine flour, salt, and black pepper in a large bowl. Add cold butter and use a pastry blender or fingers to work into a crumbly mixture.
Gradually add ice water, stirring with a fork until dough forms. Be careful not to overmix.
Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead a few times until smooth.
Wrap dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Divide dough into 8 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a tablespoon of chopped perilla-leaf-kkaennip in the center of each circle. Fold dough over filling, forming a half-moon shape, and press edges to seal.
Heat a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Cook dumplings, a few at a time, for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Transfer cooked dumplings to a baking sheet and bake in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 10-12 minutes, or until crispy and cooked through.
Serve hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
Garnish with sliced scallions and grated ginger, if desired.
